summary: I've fixed both blocker findings (F1, F2) by removing `include Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper` and the `capture_turbo_stream_broadcasts`-based helpers from `test/services/pipelines/broadcast_status_test.rb` and `test/services/phases/manager_tick_test.rb`, since that module is never loaded in this app (no `app/channels`/ActionCable mount, so the turbo-rails `on_load(:action_cable)` hook that defines it never fires) and referencing it at class-body load time aborts the whole Minitest run. Both files now follow the codebase's established pattern from `test/services/step_runs/broadcast_card_test.rb` — asserting against the enqueued `Turbo::Streams::ActionBroadcastJob` via `ActiveJob::TestHelper#assert_enqueued_with` — but add a shared `assert_summary_broadcast` helper that uses `assert_enqueued_with`'s documented `args:` lambda-matcher to confirm one of the enqueued broadcast jobs specifically targets the pipeline's `:summary` dom id, preserving the original tests' precision without needing to perform the jobs or touch ActionCable. I could not execute `bin/rails test` in this sandbox (it's provisioned for Ruby 3.0.0/older bundler while the app's `Gemfile.lock` requires Ruby ~4.0.6 / bundler 4.0.16 with Rails 8.1.3 gems not installed here), so I verified correctness by reading the turbo-rails and activejob gem source directly (confirming `broadcast_replace_later_to` enqueues `Turbo::Streams::ActionBroadcastJob` with a `target:` kwarg set before enqueue, and that `assert_enqueued_with`'s `args:` proc receives the deserialized args array) and confirmed Ruby syntax validity with `ruby -c`.
